Off Calendar Sep-05-2019 Continued To May-04-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRIKON MANAGEMENT, INC., JAI LAXMI, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendants Trikon Management, Inc. and Jai Laxmi, LLC's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant fails to maintain its initial burden of production.

The owner-operator of an elevator is a common carrier and it owes a high duty of care to those that use the machine. (See Treadwell v. Whittier (1889) 80 Cal. 574, 600 [elevator owners "must adopt the most improved modes of construction and machinery in known use in the business, and if they do not, and injury occur, they will be held responsible."]; Brown v. George Pepperdine Foundation (1943) 23 Cal.2d 256, 261 ["An owner is bound to use the utmost care and diligence in the maintenance of elevators. [Citations.] In the fulfillment of this obligation something more than regular and frequent inspections is required."]

In this case, defendant argues that it conducted regular inspections and had no actual or constructive notice of a dangerous condition. Defendant fails to maintain its burden of production. Defendant fails to show that it met its heightened duty in maintaining the elevator. Defendant admits that the pump tank nut was missing on June 2, 2016, and it was unclear whether it fell into the tank. Mr. Bruce Agid opines that the nut falling into the tank would not create a dangerous condition. The court sustains plaintiff's objection to this testimony because Mr. Agid has not been properly qualified as an expert witness. The court further notes that defendants fail to adduce any "state all facts" discovery responses that would allow the court to reasonably infer that no other defects existed in the elevator and plaintiffs cannot establish their claims. (See Scheiding v. Dinwiddie (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 84 [motion that did not rely upon "factually devoid" discovery responses from which an absence of evidence could be inferred was "insufficient to support a reasonable inference that plaintiffs could not produce further evidence to support an element crucial to their claims."]; Weber v. John Crane (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1439 [burden did not shift where defendant relied solely on plaintiff's deposition testimony and did not cite any of plaintiff's special discovery responses].)

Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
